blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I know you're kind. But you don't have to go that far. I can't prove it, but Mark hired people to interfere. That's just petty.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone doesn't want to be first.
He wasn't competing to gain numbers given by others.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He wants to pass the advancement exam and reach a higher level. He knows that advancing without being convinced himself would be meaningless.
Amy understood Shirone's feelings perfectly, which made her all the more frustrated.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But this time, luck wasn't on his side. It's too dangerous. This exam needs to be stopped here.
The Level 10 of the Uncrossable Bridge was impossible for anyone at the Class Four level to cross.
Even though Shirone was still holding on, based on Amy's experience, the difficulty of the traps he had faced so far was only around Level 6.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Uwaaaaah! Uwaaaaah!
Mark finally collapsed, his eyes rolling back as foam spilled from his mouth.
In contrast, Shirone's focus grew even sharper.
At some point, even the teachers who had been stomping their feet in frustration and the students who had been making a fuss fell silent, as if they had forgotten their own existence.
Even Alphaeus was repeatedly amazed, his eyes glued to Shirone's dazzling movements.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Crisis brings out potential. But everyone has their limits. How?

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Could he still hold on?
The Uncrossable Bridge was now racing at a speed of 200 kilometers per hour.
An ordinary student would have been overwhelmed by the speed alone and lost consciousness.
Yet, Shirone was darting through the black iron structures like lightning.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Was there never a limit from the beginning?
Even when riding a rising trend, the reason one couldn't soar endlessly was that every human eventually reached a point of self-satisfaction.
Magic was no different.
Was the reason not all magicians could become the best simply a matter of skill or talent?
The moment one reached a certain level, the self-imposed limit of satisfaction became an inevitable psychological barrier.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Shirone, are you dreaming of infinity in a human body?
It seemed as if he had no such vessel to begin with.
Because he had no standard for satisfaction, his mind could endure the endlessly rising difficulty.
Shirone, clearly in a state of ecstasy, might not even remember this moment.
But Ethela, the youngest bishop of the Carthis Council, would likely never forget Shirone's prowess she witnessed today.

blocks.speaker: Ethela
blocks.content: He's finding escape routes in real time, each with odds close to infinity.
What kind of mind could make that possible?

blocks.speaker: Ethela
blocks.content: Even great masters can only maintain such mental strength in a state of complete detachment.
The structures were now twisting into bizarre shapes, beyond complex, entering a chaotic trap where no rules could be discerned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It needs to be broken down further.
As the waves shortened to the point where moving less than a meter would be unavoidable, Shirone's jumping distance also decreased.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If he couldn't calculate each move one by one, he had to analyze everything from the start and dive in.
Left, right, left, left, right, left...
Shirone imprinted the overlapping ring-shaped traps in his mind and recalled their patterns.
As he teleported, flashes of light zigzagged through the structures, but this time, identical traps rushed in vertically.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It needs to be denser!
Time fragmented further, and the teleportation flashes curved into a parabola, bending downward.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Rainbow Drop! He's finally reached that level!
The bending flashes of light swam through the center of the circle like a loach, piercing through.
Rainbow Drop was an advanced teleportation technique, achievable only by graduation-level specialists.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Is that even possible? How can teleportation bend? Light only moves in straight lines!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It's simple. If you differentiate a circle, you get infinite straight lines. He just fragmented the teleportation intervals into thousandths.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Thousandths? Can a human do that?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Even if it's impossible to calculate, you can imagine it intuitively. He's more of an omnipotent genius than an omniscient one. In short, the opposite of me.
The students turned their heads away, as if ignoring Iruki's words.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: He's obsessed with geniuses. For a Class Five.
The Uncrossable Bridge's speed reached its peak.
The afterimages of the iron bars stretched so long that only those whose eyes had adapted could distinguish their shapes.
Shirone's movements through the afterimages transcended technique, reaching the level of art.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Could he... actually do it?
Only 30 meters remained to the bridge's endpoint.
Now, even the students set aside their thoughts and silently cheered for Shirone's success.
For the first time in the magic school's history, an upperclassman might master the Uncrossable Bridge.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Headmaster! Look there!
Someone pointed to the bridge's endpoint, and all the teachers who saw it turned pale.
The inevitable had finally arrived.
From the endpoint, the iron bars began twisting like burning hair, forming a massive three-dimensional structure.

blocks.speaker: Teacher
blocks.content: The Dragon's Labyrinth! That can't be! Disable the device now!
The Dragon's Labyrinth was a trap that appeared only once at Level 10, named for its resemblance to a dragon's face when completed.
While the exterior looked similar, the internal structure varied each time, and the iron bars used to construct it stretched a staggering 600 meters.
Without analyzing the twisting process in advance and finding an escape pattern, one would be swallowed by the dragon and left battered.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's coming!
Despite the situation, the Dragon's Labyrinth charged at them at the fastest speed ever recorded, its maw wide open.
The time to arrival was barely a second. Even Shirone couldn't help but feel his heart drop this time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Don't give up!
An electric jolt struck his brain as Shirone steadied his Spirit Zone, shaken by Mark's scream.
This much took a tenth of a second.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was there any leeway?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But while imagining dividing a second into ten was possible, experiencing 0.1 seconds ten times was not.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thus, there was only one method: keep dividing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A hundredth of a second.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He reconstructed the Dragon's Labyrinth's formation in his mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Its overall shape was a seven-pointed star. Six branching paths.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A thousandth of a second.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: After retracing the labyrinth's form, he found the escape route.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: To break free, he needed to teleport nine times in rapid succession.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No! It's impossible with Mark!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If there was no gap for two people, he had to find another route.
At that moment, the Dragon's Labyrinth swallowed Shirone.
The male students turned their heads away, while the female students screamed, imagining the gruesome outcome.
Seriel's scream was the loudest.
Whoosh—!
The sound of rushing wind in the confined space resembled the cries of Shirone and Mark.
Then, finally, silence.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A ten-thousandth of a second.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In a time too fleeting even for insight, Shirone analyzed every pattern again and found the escape route.
The teleportation flash burst out of the Dragon's Labyrinth, and Shirone, glowing brilliantly, crossed the finish line and landed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haa, haa.
Shirone set Mark down, took a few more steps, and then collapsed to his knees.
No one made a sound. The students stared at Shirone with eyes full of terror, as if they had seen a ghost.
Ethela teleported to find Siina, who had left to search for Sard. Shortly after, the mechanism deactivated, and Echo's voice rang out.

blocks.speaker: Echo
blocks.content: -Deactivating the Uncrossable Bridge's mechanism. Level Zero. Level Zero.
The iron bars slowed, and the structures that had grown like wooden vines returned to their original horizontal form, restoring the bridge to its initial state.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: What... what just happened?

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Is this even possible?

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: It's unbelievable. Level 10. And he cleared the Dragon's Labyrinth.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What? Why are they staring at me?
Though parts of his memory were blank, it was an undeniable fact that he had crossed the Uncrossable Bridge.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ugh, I really thought I was going to die.
It was a disappointingly plain statement from someone who had just made history.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Amy! Shirone did it! Your boyfriend did it!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Haha...
Amy's lips trembled.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Now, just two steps left.
Amy's feet felt just as heavy, but she needed to calm her excitement by doing something.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy.
When the two locked eyes, the surrounding students murmured in awe.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Did he just call her by name?

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Of course. They're dating.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: But even so, she's Senior Amy...
Amy, who had never lost first place, was more than just a senior to the juniors.
Given that she could easily compete at the graduation level, the current situation was astonishing.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Let's go, Seriel.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, okay.
As Amy walked away, Seriel hurried after her down the mountain.
Except for Class Seven, the other upperclassmen also began to disperse.
Finally, Sard approached Alphaeus.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Headmaster, I need to talk to you about Maria.
Sard reported everything that had happened in the control room.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: I see. How could such a mistake...

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I made an irreversible mistake. I'll accept any punishment.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: I have something to confess. Everything that happened today was because of me.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: What do you mean, because of you?

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Actually, I really got under Maria's skin this morning. Honestly, even I would've lost it if I'd heard those things. So, I'll take the punishment. I'm the cause.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Even so, Maria's actions were problematic. And why are you saying this? You could've kept quiet.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: I'm not a good guy, but I'm not petty enough to shirk responsibility. Besides, everyone already knows. And by the way, I was the one who planned to counter Senior Shirone.
Mark, who had always believed winning was all that mattered, had learned something from his battle with Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Overwhelming skill.
Shirone's focus on overcoming obstacles without distractions had shaken Mark's entire mindset.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: Anyway, that's how it is. I'll accept any punishment, but go easy on the others. Especially Maria...
Mark glanced at Maria.

blocks.speaker: Mark
blocks.content: She must've been under a lot of stress. We ganged up on her every day. Who wouldn't snap?
Alphaeus also had deep thoughts about Maria.
Though not academically outstanding, she had trained longer than anyone at the school, silently persevering.
Facing expulsion for an irreversible mistake, she reminded him of his younger self.
Master...
Sard, who knew Alphaeus's past, watched him with pity.
The light of the Mirhi family.
Alphaeus had first made a name for himself with light magic.
Though now considered outdated theory, it was an innovative idea at the time, earning him the Gold Circle Award from the king.
But his shining talent had led young Alphaeus into chaos.
If not for a single misjudgment that left him wandering in darkness for ten years, what might have been?
Perhaps he would have been a great magician etched in history.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Both of you will face the disciplinary committee. Maria illegally activated the mechanism, interfering with the exam and creating unnecessary danger. However, since Mark was the instigator, her punishment will be lenient.
As Maria and Mark bowed, Class Seven students began raising their hands one by one.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: Teacher, I bullied Maria. Doesn't that make me responsible too?

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: I was part of the plan to attack Senior Shirone.
Every Class Seven student raised their hand, showing the issue stemmed from the entire class.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: Then all of you will be punished. Return and await the committee's decision.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: What about Maria?

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: The committee will consider it. The punishment will apply to the whole class, and Maria won't bear all the blame alone.
Though not a definitive answer, the weight of the headmaster's words was undeniable.

blocks.speaker: Siina
blocks.content: Isn't that too risky? We don't know how the committee will rule.

blocks.speaker: Alphaeus
blocks.content: We're teachers. Our role is to guide students rightly, not judge them as magistrates. They're the students we must teach and nurture.

blocks.speaker: Maria
blocks.content: Thank you. I'm sorry.
Sard smiled as he watched.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Well done, Maria.
This moment might be the miraculous opportunity Maria would never get again.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Just like Sard when he met Alphaeus.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Some call it luck... but you can't even get that if you give up.
If Maria hadn't truly loved magic, could she have endured in Class Seven for so long?

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: Though the world was unfair, Sard believed it was worth living.

blocks.speaker: Sard
blocks.content: If one dreamed earnestly and persevered, opportunity would come to anyone.
